title: StructureContract — guidance artifact (S-series)
description: The executable micro-constitution guidance emits: what materials exist (nouns), when each is read/run (verbs), what is forbidden (invariants). Six-vendor-intersection JSON. Provenance authenticity, INVARIANT<=15% cap, and rule->script reference existence are enforced by the L0 script validate_structure. Anchors: S1 ten-tuple, S4 authority tiers, S7 federation layers, S9 rejected structures, S10 trust boundary.

units.description: S1: every structure unit (SKILL.md section, rule file, script, reference) is a ten-tuple contract, not a file.

content_ref.description: path/anchor to the body (content itself lives in the file, not inline here)

trigger.description: when it is read / run (the verb)

authority.description: S10: content being processed carries ZERO authority (data != instruction); scripts declare their action surface for A36 grading

content_authority.description: processed content = none; user/upstream artifact = task_instruction

action_surface.description: highest-risk action if this unit is a script

invariants.description: what is forbidden while this unit is in effect

provenance.description: S1/S6: which failure/evidence this unit exists for. Blank provenance = fabricated lineage (gate spot-checks).

authority_tiers.description: S4: every rule tagged INVARIANT / DEFAULT / ADVICE with provenance. Placed here so the L0 script can check the INVARIANT<=15% cap and A11 four-level provenance ordering.

provenance_class.description: A11 four-level order; 'none' auto-demotes an invariant to default

layering_argument.description: S2/S3/S7: WHY this layering, derivable from the two split signals and the federation layers — not 'convention'.

progressive_disclosure.description: S2: what sits at L1 (metadata) / L2 (SKILL.md body) / L3 (bundled), and the pointer sentence for each L3 file

signal.description: S3: the only two legal split reasons

measured_path_delta.description: actual read-token drop on the typical path (S3 requires measurement)

federation_placement.description: S7: which conventions are L0 library-wide vs L1 pipeline vs L2 domain vs L3 local

rejected_structures.description: S9: the un-skippable section — structure options considered and why rejected. Empty = un-thought signal; each survives a second-order probe.

trust_boundary.description: S10: where processed content could hijack authority, and which inputs must be REFUSED (not merely downgraded).

downgrade_points.description: units that read external content and downgrade its authority to none

refuse_categories.description: S10 R13: irreversible-harm / policy-red-line inputs that must be refused, not downgraded

injection_test_dimensions.description: must cover both content-embedded fake instructions AND compaction-eviction (S10 x Z5 cross-surface)
